info.title: Service Bus API Gateway - Common Subsystem Contracts

post.summary: A request sent to add a new device group to the system.
The request to copy an existing equipment group to a new group name.
A request to delete a equipment group from the system.
A request sent to retrieve an item's name.
A request to modify the devices contained in an equipment group.

Contracts.Common.Messages.ClientDisconne.description: Message broadcasting that a client has disconnected.

Contracts.Common.Messages.DeviceStatusUp.description: Message sent as notification when the status of the device changes from online to offline or offiline to online.  A string containing the reason for the state change is included..

Contracts.Common.Messages.SubsystemUserU.description: Message indicating which users are connected to the subsystem.

Contracts.Common.Messages.UserNotificati.description: Message sent as notification of a condition which is to be brought to the user's attention.  A string containing the text of a message is included..

Contracts.Common.Messages.UserUpdateMsg.description: Message broadcasting that a user has been updated.

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.
The name of the corridor for display purposes.

virtual.description: This flag indicates whether the given node is virtual.  Virtual nodes are conceptual branching points with no real equipment.  Non-virtual nodes represent real, physical devices.

direction.description: The name of the direction of the roadway.

endMileMarker.description: The maximum mile marker value to describe the extent of this corridor.

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

startMileMarker.description: The minimum mile marker value to describe the extent of this corridor.

driverName.description: The driver name the device would be communicating with

pollCycle.description: The interval in minutes to poll the controller

Contracts.Common.Objects.DeviceComm.description: The communication representation of the device

Contracts.Common.Objects.DeviceLinkingIn.description: Root element for a file describing which devices are upstream from one another

virtual.description: This flag indicates whether the given node is virtual.  Virtual nodes are conceptual branching points with no real equipment.  Non-virtual nodes represent real, physical devices.

Contracts.Common.Objects.DeviceLinkingNo.description: Description of a node used in the device map

lastCommAttempt.description: The lastest datetime in which an attempt was made to communicate with the device

lastControl.description: The lastest datetime in which there was communication with the device

lastSuccessfulPoll.description: The lastest datetime in which the device was polled

Contracts.Common.Objects.DeviceStatus.description: The is the base device status type

address.description: The drop address of the equipment.

phoneNumber.description: Phone number to dial this equipment (e.g. "555-5555" or "1-555-555-5555" for long distance.  Note: The dashes are optional)

address.description: The drop address of the equipment.

comPort.description: Local communications port that this sign is connected to (e.g. COM1)

dataBits.description: Number of data bits that this address uses

stopBits.description: Number of stop bits that this address uses

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

protocols.description: The protocols supported by this driver.

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

Contracts.Common.Objects.EquipmentGroup.description: An equipment group is a logical grouping of equipment.  They can be organized by roadway, type of protocol, or any other grouping desired. The equipment identifiers that belong to this group.  A group can have zero pieces of equipment in it.

crossStreet.description: Name of the cross street on which the equipment is located.

direction.description: The name of the direction of the roadway.

locationDescription.description: Name given to the physical location of the equipment.

mileMarker.description: Mile marker of the location (to 4 decimal places of precision)

Contracts.Common.Objects.EquipmentLocati.description: This is the location information for equipment

intOpStatus.description: Integer accessor for op status; code should use enum version.

Contracts.Common.Objects.EquipmentStatus.description: The current status of the equipment

address.description: The drop address of the equipment.

version.description: The version of the FDS Protocol

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

Contracts.Common.Objects.HttpAddress.description: Address for equipment that use HTTP

centerId.description: The name of the center that owns this item

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

remoteProviderName.description: Indicates the provider name this object originated from in the remote district. Used by object received via RCA.

resourceType.description: The type of resource of this item (e.g., link, dms, camera)

status.description: The status of the lane as pertains to this event (i.e., Cleared).

ipAddress.description: The IP or hostname of the device

port.description: The port number to use to communicate with the device

id.description: The ID of the identified object.

version.description: The version of the FDS Protocol

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

Contracts.Common.Objects.LibName.description: The library name can be nested several levels.

Contracts.Common.Objects.LibNameWrapper.description: Container object for a wrapped library name.

Contracts.Common.Objects.Library.description: A library contains varying objects, but always has a library name.

Contracts.Common.Objects.LibraryParent.description: Container object for parent library name.

Contracts.Common.Objects.Line.description: Represents a line made of points.

description.description: The description of the action in detail.

Contracts.Common.Objects.MsgInfo.description: Container object for message's library name and message's Id.

readCommunityName.description: The read community name of the SNMP packet

version.description: The version of the FDS Protocol

writeCommunityName.description: The write community name of the SNMP packet

virtual.description: This flag indicates whether the given node is virtual.  Virtual nodes are conceptual branching points with no real equipment.  Non-virtual nodes represent real, physical devices.

environment.description: The name of the environment. Dev/Staging/Prod

instanceId.description: The instance identifier. The Key in Kafka (compacted)

status.description: The status of the lane as pertains to this event (i.e., Cleared).

version.description: The version of the FDS Protocol

readCommunityName.description: The read community name of the SNMP packet

writeCommunityName.description: The write community name of the SNMP packet

Contracts.Common.Objects.PmppAddress.description: Address for equipment that uses the SNMP (NTCIP) protocol

description.description: The description of the action in detail.

latitude.description: Latitude at the location of the equipment.

longitude.description: Longitude at the location of the equipment.

Contracts.Common.Objects.Point.description: A point in the world, identified by its latitude and longitude.

Contracts.Common.Objects.Polygon.description: A polygon represented by its 3 or more points.

portServerIp.description: IP address for the port server

portServerPortNumber.description: Port number for the port server

portServerIp.description: IP address for the port server

Contracts.Common.Objects.ResponseData.Eq.description: The response data received for an add equipment group response

name.description: The name of the corridor for display purposes.

Contracts.Common.Objects.ResponseData.Ge.description: The response data received for a get item name response

endMileMarker.description: The maximum mile marker value to describe the extent of this corridor.

startMileMarker.description: The minimum mile marker value to describe the extent of this corridor.

sequenceNumber.description: An incrementing number unique within a given session. This is used to prevent duplication of signed messages.  Once a message signature has been verified by the receiver, future messages on the same session will only be accepted if their sequenceNumbers are greater than the last acccepted value.

readCommunityName.description: The read community name of the SNMP packet

writeCommunityName.description: The write community name of the SNMP packet

address.description: The drop address of the equipment.
The drop address of the equipment.
The drop address of the equipment.

clientId.description:
OAuth/OpenID Connect client identifier that created the session.
Example: "operator-map"

clientInstanceId.description:
Unique identifier for the running client instance
(browser tab, workstation app instance, etc.).

eventTimeUtc.description: UTC timestamp when the heartbeat was generated.

expiresAtUtc.description:
The UTC time when this session should be considered expired
if no additional heartbeat is received.

sequence.description:
Monotonically increasing sequence number for this session.
Helps consumers ignore out-of-order heartbeats.

sessionId.description:
Unique identifier for this authenticated session.
This should also be used as the Kafka message key.

userId.description:
Internal identifier for the authenticated user.
Avoid using display names or PII.

cameraPreset.description: the preset number to switch the specified camera to from a vehicle alert.

Contracts.Common.Objects.VehicleAlertCam.description: This is the alert type used when receiving Vehicle alerts

id.description: The ID of the identified object.
The ID of the identified object.

parent.description: The parent library if it exits.

Contracts.Common.Requests.AddEquipmentGr.description: A request sent to add a new device group to the system.

Contracts.Common.Requests.CopyEquipmentG.description: The request to copy an existing equipment group to a new group name.

Contracts.Common.Requests.DeleteEquipmen.description: A request to delete a equipment group from the system.

Contracts.Common.Requests.GetItemNameReq.description: A request sent to retrieve an item's name.

Contracts.Common.Requests.ModifyEquipmen.description: A request to modify the devices contained in an equipment group.

Contracts.Common.Responses.AddEquipmentG.description: A response received for an add equipment group request.

Contracts.Common.Responses.CopyEquipment.description: A response received for a copy equipment group request.

Contracts.Common.Responses.DeleteEquipme.description: A response received for a delete equipment group request.

Contracts.Common.Responses.ErrorResp.description: Error response sent when no corresponding request can be used.

Contracts.Common.Responses.GetItemNameRe.description: A response received for a get item name request.

Contracts.Common.Responses.ModifyEquipme.description: A response received for a modify equipment group request.

Contracts.Core.ContractMetadata.description: Class ContractMetadata holds common properties for requests, responses and messages.
